methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Dream Security, a Series B defense and security company founded by Shalev Hulio and Sebastian Kurz, is valued at $1.5B post-money following $100M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage, including Vannevar Labs ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Castelion ($1.0B), Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), and Heven AeroTech ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression modeling applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $1.5B last post-money valuation, implying $2.9B. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector Series B comparables yields a median of $1.0B (range $1.0B–$1.5B). This discrepancy reflects Dream Security's position at the upper end of its peer set.

The triangulated valuation range is $850M (low) to $3.3B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The current $1.5B valuation sits below the median, aligning with the peer-median method but below stage-progression expectations. Key uncertainties include Dream Security's specific revenue and growth trajectory relative to the peer set, and whether the 1.9x stage multiplier appropriately reflects sector-specific capital dynamics in frontier defense technology.
